questions.q: A surveyor on one bank of canal observed the image of the 4 inch and 17 ft marks on a vertical staff, which is partially immersed in the water and held against the bank directly opposite to him, coincides. If the 17 ft mark and the surveyor's eye are both 6 ft above the water level, estimate the width of the canal, assuming that the refractive index of the water is $4 / 3$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The final image I of the object $O$ shown in the figure is formed at point 20 cm below a thin equal-concave lens, which is at a depth of 65 cm from principal axis. From the given geometry, calculate the radius of curvature in cm of lens kept at "A".
questions.solution:
Image formation due to convex lens $\frac{1}{V}-\frac{1}{-36}=\frac{1}{30} \quad \Rightarrow v=\frac{30 \times 36}{6}=180 \mathrm{~cm}$ This image will act like a virtual object for mirror and after reflection from mirror its image (shown by I2) will be formed at 80 cm below optical axis of convex lens. For concave lens, this image will be object at a position of 15 cm below the lens. For final image formed by concave lens. $\frac{1}{20}-\frac{1}{15}=\frac{1}{f} \quad \Rightarrow \frac{1}{f}=-\frac{5}{300}$
Also, $\frac{1}{\mathrm{f}}=(\mu-1)\left(-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}}\right)$ Or $-\frac{5}{300}=\left(\frac{3}{2}-1\right)\left(\frac{-2}{R}\right) \Rightarrow R=\frac{300}{5}$ Ans. Radius of curvature $=60 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two persons $A$ and $B$ wear glasses of optical powers (in air) $P_1=+2 D$ and $P_2=+1 D$ respectively. The glasses have refractive index 1.5 . Now they jump into a swimming pool and look at each other. B appears to be present at distance $2 \mathrm{~m}($ from A$)$ to A . 'A' appears to be present at distance 1 m (from $B$ ) to $B$. Find out the refractive index of water in the swimming pool, in the from $\frac{X}{10}$ and fill value of $X$.
questions.solution:
We have $f_1=50 \mathrm{~cm}$ and $f_2=100 \mathrm{~cm}$ Let the real distance between $A$ and $B$ be $x$. Also let refractive index of liquid be $\mu$. Then
$\frac{1}{f_1}=\left(\frac{3}{2}-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right) \quad \Rightarrow \quad\left(\frac{1}{R_1}-\frac{1}{R_2}\right)=\frac{2}{f_1}$
$\frac{1}{f_1}=\left(\frac{3}{2 \mu}-1\right)\left(\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_1}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_2}\right) \quad \Rightarrow \quad \frac{1}{f_1}=\frac{2}{f_1}\left(\frac{3-2 \mu}{2 \mu}\right)$
And $\frac{1}{\mathrm{f}_2^{\prime}}=\frac{2}{\mathrm{f}_2}\left(\frac{3-2 \mu}{2 \mu}\right)$
Now, for A we have $-\left(\frac{1}{200}\right)-\left(\frac{1}{-x}\right)=\frac{2}{50}\left(\frac{3-2 \mu}{2 \mu}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{1}{x}=\frac{1}{200}+\frac{2}{50}\left(\frac{3-2 \mu}{2 \mu}\right)$...(1)
Also for B we have
$-\frac{1}{100}-\left(-\frac{1}{x}\right)=\frac{2}{100}\left(\frac{3-2 \mu}{2 \mu}\right)$
so $\frac{1}{x}=\frac{1}{100}+\frac{2}{100}\left(\frac{3-2 \mu}{2 \mu}\right)$...(2)
from (1) and (2) we get
$\Rightarrow \frac{2(3-2 \mu)}{100(2 \mu)}+\frac{1}{100}=\frac{1}{200}+\frac{2(3-2 \mu)}{50(2 \mu)}$
$\Rightarrow \mu=12 / 10$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A light ray parallel to the principal axis is incident (as shown in the figure) on a thin planoconvex lens with radius of curvature of its curved part equal to 10 cm . Assuming that the refractive index of the material of the lens is $4 / 3$ and medium on both sides of the lens is air, find the distance of the point from the lens where this ray meets the principal axis. Find your answer in the form $X / 7 \mathrm{~cm}$ and fill value of $X$.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{R}=10 \mathrm{~cm}$
Applying snell's law $\frac{\sin \theta}{\sin r}=\frac{3}{4} \quad \Rightarrow r=53^{\circ}$ By sine law in $\triangle A B C \frac{\sin (r-\theta)}{10}=\frac{\sin (\pi-r)}{(10+x)}$ $\frac{10+x}{10}=\frac{4}{5(\sin r \cos \theta-\cos r \sin \theta)}$ $=\frac{4}{5\left(\frac{4}{5} \times \frac{4}{5}-\frac{3}{5} \times \frac{3}{5}\right)}$ $\Rightarrow 10+x=\frac{200}{7}$ $\Rightarrow x=\frac{200-70}{7}=\frac{130}{7}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A thin equi-convex lens of refractive index $3 / 2$ is placed on a horizontal plane mirror as shown in the figure. The space between the lens and the mirror is then filled with water of refractive index $4 / 3$. It is found that when a point object is placed 15 cm above the lens on its principle axis, the object coincides with its own image. On repeating with another liquid, the object and the image again coincide at a distance 25 cm from the lens. Calculate the refractive index of the liquid.
questions.solution:
RI of lens material $=\mu_2 ;$ RI of first liquid $=\mu_3 ;$ RI of air $=\mu_1 ;$ RI of second liquid $=\mu_3^{\prime}$
In general $\left(\mu_3 / v\right)-\left(\mu_1 / u\right)=\left\{\left(\mu_2-\mu_1\right) / R\right\}+\left\{\left(\mu_3-\mu_2\right) / R\right\}$
Here $\mathbf{u}=-15 \mathrm{~cm}, \mathrm{v}=\infty$
Now, $1 /(+15)=[(3 / 2)-1\} / R]-[\{(4 / 3)-(3 / 2)\} / R]$
$\Rightarrow R=10 \mathrm{~cm}$
For the second liquid, similarly,
$(1 / 25)=(0.5 / R)-\left[\left\{\mu^{\prime}{ }_3-(3 / 2)\right\} / R\right]$
$\Rightarrow \mu^{\prime}{ }_3=1.6$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The size of the image of an object, which is at infinity, as formed by a convex lens of focal length 30 cm is 2 cm . Now a concave lens of focal length 20 cm is placed between the convex lens and image at a distance of 26 cm from the convex lens. The new size of the image is $x \times 10^{-1} \mathrm{~cm}$. Find the value of $x$.
questions.solution:
In First case $\theta=\frac{2}{30}=\frac{1}{15} \mathrm{rad}$
In second case, image is formed in focal plane of concave lens 20 cm left of concave lens of size, Size of image $=20 \times \frac{1}{15}=\frac{4}{3} \mathrm{~cm}$ $\frac{1}{v_2}+\frac{1}{46}=\frac{1}{30} \Rightarrow v=\frac{46 \times 30}{16}$ $\mathrm{m}=\frac{46 \times 30}{16} \Rightarrow \mathrm{~h}=\frac{46 \times 30}{46 \times 16} \times 4 / 3=2.5 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A concave mirror of radius of curvature 40 cm is filled with water $(n=4 / 3)$ upto a height of 12 cm . A point object O is kept on the principal axis of the mirror at height 13.5 cm from the water surface. The final image formed w.r.t water surface (in cm ) after refraction at water surface, reflection at mirror and again refraction at water surface in succession is $\qquad$ .
questions.solution:
$P^{\prime}=13.5 \times 4 / 3=18 \mathrm{~cm}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{v_2}+\frac{1}{v_2}=\frac{1}{f} \\ & \frac{1}{v_2}-\frac{1}{30}=-\frac{1}{20}\end{aligned}$ $v_2=-60 \mathrm{~cm}$ from point, $P$ final image gets formed at $=(60-12) \times 3 / 4=36 \mathrm{~cm}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A person runs with a speed $u$ towards a bicycle moving away from him with speed $v$. The person approaches his image in the mirror fixed at the rear bicycle with a speed of $x(u-v)$. Calculate the value of $x$.
questions.solution: Velocity of approach of man towards the bicycle $=(u-v)$ Hence velocity of approach of image towards bicycle is $2(u-v)$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The $x z$ plane is the boundary between two transparent medium. Medium $-I$ with $y \geq 0$ has refractive index $\mu$ and medium $-I I$ with $\mathrm{y} \leq 0$ has a refractive index 1. A ray of light in medium-I, given by vector $\vec{n}=\hat{i}-\sqrt{3} \hat{j}$ is incident on the plane of separation. If the reflected and refracted rays make an angle of $90^{\circ}$ with each other, then the value of $\mu$ is $\sqrt{k}$. Find the value of $k$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small object at P oscillates infront of a concave mirror as shown in the figure perpendicular to principal axis according to equation. $y=4 \sin \left(\omega t+\frac{\pi}{3}\right) c \mathrm{~cm}$ y is the displacement of particle perpendicular to principal axis. The amplitude (in cm ) of image is
questions.solution:
$\frac{1}{v}+\frac{1}{u}=\frac{1}{f}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{v}-\frac{1}{(3 R / 2)}=-\frac{2}{R} \\ & \frac{1}{v}=-\frac{2}{R}+\frac{2}{3 R}=-\frac{4}{3 R} \\ & v=-\frac{3 R}{4} \\ & m=-\frac{v}{u}=-\frac{\left(-\frac{3 R}{4}\right)}{\left(-\frac{3 R}{2}\right)}=-\frac{2}{4}=-\frac{1}{2} \\ & \text { amplitude of image }=4\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two transparent slabs have the same thickness as shown. One is made of material $A$ of refractive index 1.5 . The other is made of two materials $B$ and $C$ with thickness in the ratio $1: 2$. The refractive index of $C$ is 1.6 . If a monochromatic parallel beam passing through the slabs has the same number of wavelengths inside both, the refractive index of $B$ is $\frac{10+x}{10}$. Then $x$ is:
questions.solution:
For A :
Total number of waves $=\frac{(1.5) \mathrm{t}}{\lambda}$  ...(i)
$$
\because \quad\binom{\text { Totalnumber }}{\text { of waves }}=\left(\frac{\text { optical path length }}{\text { wavelength }}\right)
$$
For $B$ and $C$ :
Total number of waves $=\frac{n_B\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)}{\lambda}+\frac{(1.6)\left(\frac{2 t}{3}\right)}{\lambda}$   ...(ii)
Equating (1) and (2)
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{B}} \quad=1.3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
